text.title: Notion PM Failure Modes
text.summary: 4 antipatterns observed when applying notion-pm. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix keyed to the methodology.

antipattern.symptom: Page load takes 5-8s; "Completed Tasks" formula returns wrong counts or errors.
antipattern.root-cause: Formula tries to iterate prop("Tasks") relation and check each task's Status manually; not supported.
antipattern.fix: Replace with a Rollup: relation Tasks → property Status → calculation Count values filtered to "Done". Formulas may post-process the Rollup result only.

antipattern.symptom: Rollup counts drift after housekeeping; tasks vanish from sprint without status change recorded.
antipattern.root-cause: Deleted (hard-removed) pages break rollups pointing to them; the system has no warning.
antipattern.fix: Always set Status = "Cancelled" or use archived=true via API instead of deleting. Preserves referential integrity for Rollups.

antipattern.symptom: Bulk script "succeeds" (200 responses) but UI shows only some updates; state drift.
antipattern.root-cause: notion-client SDK does not raise on 429 by default; calls are silently throttled.
antipattern.fix: Cap writes at 3 req/sec, check status code explicitly, exponential backoff on 429, require human checkpoint for >50 bulk mutations.

antipattern.symptom: API write returns 200 but property is not updated; agent moves on; data inconsistent.
antipattern.root-cause: Property names in API calls must match the UI display name exactly (case + spacing). Silent failure on mismatch.
antipattern.fix: Resolve the canonical property name once from databases.retrieve, cache it, assert exact string match before writes.
